pi_items.source: Task setup script (task_utils.sh) and environment definitions.
Task setup script (setup_task.sh) clearing and creating this directory.
Code execution on simulated Muncie HDF5 results showing peak upstream flow of 21,000 cfs, which is > 1000.
Code execution on simulated Muncie HDF5 results showing peak upstream flow of 21,000 cfs, which is < 50000.
Code execution showing actual attenuation is ~0.01%, which is >= 0.
Code execution showing actual attenuation is ~0.01%, which is <= 50.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates on the standard Muncie, Indiana (White River) HEC-RAS example dataset. Code execution to run the HEC-RAS unsteady simulation and parse the resulting HDF5 file (Muncie.p04.hdf) revealed a peak upstream flow of ~21,000 cfs and a peak downstream flow of ~20,998 cfs. This confirms a positive flood peak attenuation of ~0.01%, a travel lag of ~5.17 hours, and an ~8.8% volume difference. All metadata constraints (min/max bounds and file paths) correctly bound and describe the expected outputs for this specific geographic simulation.
